whatThisIs: Show the adult cardiac arrest drug reference card straight from the AHA ACLS algorithm: epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO every 3-5 minutes for non-shockable and persistent shockable rhythms; amiodarone 300 mg IV/IO first dose, 150 mg IV/IO second dose for refractory VF / pulseless VT; lidocaine 1-1.5 mg/kg first dose, 0.5-0.75 mg/kg subsequent (max 3 mg/kg) as an alternative; magnesium 1-2 g IV/IO for torsades; calcium chloride and sodium bicarbonate for the specific reversible-cause indications. Sophie Well lays the doses and intervals out as a single card for code-team reference.
whenToUse: Use this at the bedside or in a code-team debrief when the team needs a quick reread of the ACLS drug doses without paging through the algorithm flowchart, or when teaching a learner the difference between routine ACLS pushes and the reversible-cause adjuncts. The reference does not replace the algorithm - CPR quality, rhythm check timing, and reversible-cause search are still the work; the drug doses are the easy part.
